title: oscore.json: protection vectors consumed by NO implementation; Rust key_derivation is symmetry-only; C has no crypto vectors
description: Python tests/crypto/test_oscore_vectors.py consumes key_derivation (full expected-value checks) and replay; its roundtrip test only checks key symmetry - never protects/unprotects, never touches request_protection/response_protection. Rust lichen-oscore/tests/vectors.rs: roundtrip is protect->unprotect self-oracle (the anti-pattern that let 2auf.48.3 survive), key_derivation checks symmetry only (never compares expected sender_key/recipient_key/common_iv, lines 234-281) and SKIPS all id_context vectors (:251); request/response protection types never filtered for. Zephyr C lichen/tests/oscore: context lifecycle/NVM mocks only - NO test calls oscore_protect_request/unprotect anywhere (grep-verified), so the mmgu.1 nonce bug and the yu2d AAD fix are both untestable by the current C suite. This gap is what hid two P0 nonce bugs. Fix: wire RFC C.4-C.8 protect/unprotect vectors into all three suites (after fixing the vector fields per mmgu.12), make Rust key_derivation compare expected values incl. id_context, add C crypto tests. The repo's own grind prompt (.claude/prompts/grind-remaining.md:107-113) already lists 'Add missing vector loaders'.
